title: Ubuntu install guide: can't execute Podman
body:
Following the [install guide](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/install.md) for Ubuntu (18.04.2), I end up in a rather unusable state as some configs are not installed.

```
valentin@uvm:~$ podman run alpine ls
Error: error creating libpod runtime: Error running podman info while refreshing state: exit status 1
valentin@uvm:~$ sudo podman run alpine ls
Error: unable to pull alpine: error getting default registries to try: unable to parse the registries.conf file: open /etc/containers/registries.conf: no such file or directory
```

I think the package must be updated. Let's use the issue as a tracker.

comments.body: @lsm5, can you have a look? I am not sure if it's a known issue or not. I just installed an Ubuntu VM for some performance tests.

Known issue, but I don't think we've collated all of this in the same place before.

I walked someone on IRC through getting things working a few weeks back, and we encountered the following issues:

- No dependency on `uidmap` package (needed for rootless)
- Missing `slirp4netns` (no package)
- Missing `registries.conf`
@lsm5 was looking at this package today.  Is this issue fixed?
sorry, been fighting some rhel fires, but this is on my todo for today, closing this as duplicate of https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/2116
